title: Tax Effect: If a firm has a marginal tax rate of 0.3...
summary: Calculate the tax saving from interest expense using the marginal tax rate.
prompt: If a firm has a marginal tax rate of 30% and an interest expense of $1,000, how much tax saving does it generate from this expense?
solution: The tax saving generated from interest expense can be calculated using the formula: Tax Saving = Interest Expense × Marginal Tax Rate. In this case, it would be $1,000 × 30% = $300. This tax shield is a significant benefit of using debt financing.
